title: Duplicate include in top and sub module causes verilator segmentation fault
body:
I encounter this issue when trying to make systemverilog package right. I find that, if I include a package file with:
1. `include in top module
2. `include in sub module
3. import in top module

will cause segmentation fault in verilator.

The [link to the repository](https://github.com/yodalee/test_verilator) that reproduces the issue.

The steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Clone the repository
5. cd test_verilator && mkdir build && cd build
6. cmake ..

You should see the error message of:
```
CMake Error at /usr/share/verilator/verilator-config.cmake:239 (message):
  Verilator command failed (return code=Segmentation fault)
Call Stack (most recent call first):
  CMakeLists.txt:10 (verilate)
```

The OS is Archlinux.

The verilator version:
Verilator 5.006 2023-01-22 rev UNKNOWN.REV
The master branch also has this issue.
AFAIK, the verilator 4.228 is highly possible with same issue.

I can have a look at this issue. I think it is not a serious issue, since no one is gonna use sv package in this way.
